title: angophora lanceolata var. woodsiana
intro: Angophora lanceolata var. woodsiana, also known as the coastal rose or paperbark, is a small to medium-sized tree native to eastern Australia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Angophora lanceolata var. woodsiana include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help to fight off infections. Angophora lanceolata var. woodsiana can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
